title: activity — model traits
description: Trait contract this module owns (`LogsActivity`, `BelongsToActivity`) and consumes from foundation / tenancy / vendor. `LogsActivity` is the primary extension point — every domain aggregate that wants user-facing activity capture composes it and configures `getActivitylogOptions()`.

owned.description: Composed on domain models that want user-facing activity capture. Wraps `Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity` — the vendor trait provides the machinery; this trait adds three Academorix-flavoured guarantees. (1) The activity's `tenant_id` matches the model's `tenant_id` on every write (safety cross-check pre-persist). (2) The activity's `application_id` is auto-filled from request context. (3) The composing model's `getActivitylogOptions()` must set a `log_name` in the module's registered catalog OR the trait throws at model boot — this catches typos in log_name references at deploy time instead of run time.

eventHooks: activityLogAttributes booted() hook wires tenant_id + application_id
boot-time log_name validation against the registered catalog

relations.name: activities
relations.description: All activities targeting this row as subject. Ordered by created_at DESC. Consumed by /api/v1/activities/by-entity/{type}/{id}.

relations.name: recentActivities
relations.description: Activities in the last 30 days. Powers the sidebar 'What's new on this thing' widget.

methods.name: getActivitylogOptions
methods.description: Vendor contract method. Composing model MUST override to return a `LogOptions` with `useLogName()` + `logOnly()` + `setDescriptionForEvent()` configured. Base default is unsafe (logs everything as 'default' log_name) — the trait's boot() refuses the default at first use.

methods.name: activityFor
methods.description: Convenience — read activities on this subject, optionally filtered by log_name. Wraps the `activities` relation.

methods.name: hasActivity
methods.description: Quick check — did this model receive an activity with the given event key? Powers 'this booking was cancelled' badges without a full activity fetch.

notAppliedBy: audit-adjacent aggregates — Role, Permission, Grant use HasAudit instead. LogsActivity is for user-facing feeds; audit is for regulator evidence.
PII-heavy aggregates — Profile, MedicalRecord, Payment method use HasAudit + field-level encryption. Activity descriptions are user-visible narrative and MUST NOT surface PII.
System-plane aggregates — Tenant, Application, PlatformUser — those live in the audit stream exclusively.

owned.description: Composed on rows created BY an activity (e.g. a notification triggered by an activity, a follow-up task). Adds a nullable `activity_id` FK + BelongsTo relation for provenance / traceability. Optional — most modules don't need it.
appliedBy: notifications::UserNotification rows created in response to an activity (e.g. 'notify me when this booking is cancelled')

consumed.note: Applied to ActivityRetentionPolicy (native table). NOT applied to Activity (vendor bigint id in Wave 6).

consumed.description: Applies the tenant global scope + auto-fills tenant_id on save. Registered FIRST in the composition chain.

created_by / updated_by / deleted_by on ActivityRetentionPolicy. NOT applied to Activity — the vendor table doesn't have those columns, and adding them would leak into every activity row unnecessarily (the `causer` already carries actor identity).

Emits audit rows on ActivityRetentionPolicy CRUD. Composes on the retention policy model because policy changes are AUDIT events (retention decisions are compliance-worthy). NOT applied to Activity itself — activity IS the tenant's audit surface; auditing it would be recursive.
Query-string filter support for the list endpoints — filter[log_name], filter[causer_id], filter[subject_type], filter[event], filter[from], filter[to].
Full-text search over `description` for admin dashboards. Applied to Activity only.
Applied to ActivityRetentionPolicy only. Activity does NOT soft-delete — the retention pipeline hard-deletes rows on the schedule.

compositionExample.note: Consumer example — Booking model composes LogsActivity.
